findings.evidence: New defs at lines 34-39 are exactly coercions of `selfAdjointPart ℝ A` and `skewAdjointPart ℝ A`; Mathlib defines `selfAdjointPart : A →ₗ[R] selfAdjoint A` as `(⅟2 : R) • (x + star x)` and `skewAdjointPart` as `(⅟2 : R) • (x - star x)` in `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/Star/Module.lean:130-147`, and matrices have `star := conjTranspose` in `mathlib/Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/ConjTranspose.lean:405-407`, so over `ℝ` this is `(A + Aᵀ)/2` and `(A - Aᵀ)/2`.
findings.fix: Delete these aliases and state downstream results using `(selfAdjointPart ℝ A : Matrix n n ℝ)` and `(skewAdjointPart ℝ A : Matrix n n ℝ)`, with local lemmas only where needed.
findings.issue: `symmetricPart` and `skewPart` are just matrix-valued aliases for existing `selfAdjointPart`/`skewAdjointPart`, adding parallel API for the same decomposition.

summary: The new symmetric/skew matrix decomposition is direct duplicate API for Mathlib's existing star-module self/skew-adjoint decomposition on real square matrices.
